ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Rowena Navia, Esq., for and with Leo Bautista, Successor Trustee.

At the court's query regarding whether she has seen the Examiner's notes, counsel advises of a recently filed declaration in support of the instant petition, which she summarizes for the court. Counsel confirms that the proposed successor Trustee is related to Mr. Bautista and there is discussion regarding whether the Beneficiaries had been previously advised of the relationship. Counsel confirms that the parties have not been advised of Mr. Bautista's relationship with Krystal Bautista (his daughter) and offers her reasons for not having done so.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Chwin. Not Reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Continued To Apr-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-21-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Leo Bautista (trustee) by zoom.

The court questions the request for the trustee's fees stating that 1% is about $29,000.00, that the time sheets show 63 hours of service that was provided, that at $145.00 an hour that's a little more than $9000, and that there is a big discrepancy. Mrs. Navia stated that in the last accounting the request for statutory fees was similar, that they were awarded in full, that the services that were rendered were similar and that nothing has really changed. Mrs. Navia also stated that things became a little more complicated during COVID, that a lot of the time spent by the trustee is not reported, that this is due to the fact that there's a very, very difficult beneficiary in this matter, and that Mr. Bautista stepped in for another private professional fiduciary who was unable to manage the administration of the trust. In addition, Mrs. Navia stated that they followed what they thought was the course of conduct that the court was using with regard to her client's fees. The Second Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc., is under submission with the court's intent to modify the trustee's fees.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nancy Field for and with Anne Smith.

The Court inquired what was the Trustee's rate in the last accounting and why it was raised to $160.00 this time. Ms. Smith stated that she always billed on a tier basis, and delegated tasks to her staff when she was able to keep cost lower for her clients, that she raised her hourly rate to $160 several years ago, and that the average hourly fees is less than $160.00. The Court stated that it is extraordinary for this court to go above $150.00. Ms. Smith stated that she was not aware of this. Ms. Field raised inquiry about Probate Examiner's notes regarding timesheet from the local rule, stating that she thought the rule applied to the attorney and not the Trustee, and that the local rule is a bit confusing.

The petition for final account and report of successor trustee Anne F. Smith, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Ms. Smith'S Final Account
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Off Calendar

Fourth Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Settlement Of Account, For Order Accepting Resignation Of Present Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Field for and with Anne Smith.
Rowena Navia for and with Leo Bautista (proposed Sucessor Trustee).
Michael Dobrov for and with Janet Molinari.
Suasn McElhinney appeared by telephone for Joan Decker (Objector).

Ms. Field stated that she has spoken with Ms. McElhinney, that it is her understanding that Ms. Decker is withdrawing her petition, and informed the Court that Ms. Smith is waiving her costs, her travel time and her Assistant's fees. Ms. Field also stated that she does not have the order establishing the trust, that she is hoping that someone else has it and that she filed a declaration on December 2nd addressing the trust documents. Mr. Dobrov stated that his Client is withdrawing her objections, and that she approves of the appointment of Mr. Baustista and the resignation of Ms. Smith. Ms. McElhinney stated that her Client is withdrawing her objections, that her Client agrees to Mr. Bautista being appointed and requested that the Court order that Mr. Bautista has access to the property. The Court informed Ms. McElhinney that her request is not before the Court. Mr. Dobrov stated that he has discussed this issue with his Client, that there will be cooperation and that he will file a substitution of Attorney. The Court granted the Fourth Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement of Account, for Order Accepting Resignation of Present Trustee (Anne Smith), for Appointment of Successor Trustee (Leo Bautista), for Fees, etc. (subject to review of the order). Bond for Mr. Bautista is set at $231,000.00. Status on filing of bond is set for January 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of Ms. Smith's final account is set for December 31, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of Successor Trustee is set for February 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court wants the Parties to provide the order that creates the terms of the trust.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Fourth Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Settlement Of Account, For Order Accepting Resignation Of Present Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-06-2019 Continued To Dec-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nancy Field for and with Anne Smith (Trustee).
Melanie Emmons for Leo Bautista.
Janet Molinari in propria persona (Objector).
Susan McElhinney for Joan Decker via Court Call.

The Court noted this matter is on calendar for a 4th Account and Report, that there are some questions, and inquired if Ms. Molinari is here to object to this petition. Ms. Molinari stated she is here to object and has filed her objections on July 9, 2019. The Court noted there are no filed objections dated July 9, 2019 for this petition and informed Ms. Molinari that her documents need to be filed in the correct case number. Ms. Field stated that Ms. Molinari's filing was indeed in the old case number, that she has read it, and that Ms. Molinari doesn't necessarily object to the accounting petition but that she wants repairs to the house to be done. Ms. Molinari stated that she is requesting the court's instruction for a special administrator to be appointed by the trustee to administer her mother's estate and that certain beneficiaries cannot agree which is causing detriment to the estate. The Court noted Ms. Molinari is mixing up terms as there is no administrator in trust cases and recommends Ms. Molinari to obtain an attorney. Ms. Field addresses the Court's questions, stating she filed 2 declarations late last night, that there were File and Serve Xpress fees with the court filing fees, that her client has agreed not to charge for travel time for her assistant which reduces compensation to the client by $117.00, and an increased bond has been filed and a courtesy copy will be provided to dept 202. Ms. McElhinney stated she made attempts to talk to Ms. Molinari about a professional fiduciary other than Mr. Bautista and that the other beneficiaries are in agreement except for Ms. Molinari. The Court noted Ms. Decker's rights to nominate a successor trustee is at issue, that the current trustee has a right to resign, that Ms. McElhinney should talk to Ms. Field to work out the successor issue or get something filed as necessary, that Ms. Molinari is encouraged to get an attorney and get something filed to address this pending petition, and it does no good to leave Mr. Bautista and the property to be left in limbo.

The Fourth Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for Settlement of Account, for Order Accepting Resignation of Present Trustee, for Appointment of Successor Trustee, for Fees and Costs for Trustee, for Fees and Costs for Attorney for Trustee, and for Increase in Bond is continued to December 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for any documents to be filed by November 26, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
